content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


May 2, 1990


John P. Kivi
4529 Cahuengo Court
Fair Oaks, CA 95628

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-90-279


Dear Mr. Kivi:

You have requested advice concerning the lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12 under the Political Reform Act.1 During our telephone conversation of April 23, 1990, you stated that you are not authorized by the American Federation of Musicians Local 12 to seek this advice. In that case, we must decline to issue advice to you because the Commission is prohibited from providing advice to a third party about the duties or obligations of another person or entity. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) However, I hope the following general information will be of assistance.

The term "lobbyist" is defined in Section 82039 as follows:

"Lobbyist" means any individual who is employed or contracts for economic consideration, other than reimbursement for reasonable travel expenses, to communicate directly or through his or her agents with any elective state official, agency official or legislative official for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action, if a substantial or regular portion of the activities for which he or she receives consideration is for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.


1 Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

John Kivi
page 2


     Further clarification of this definition is provided in
Regulation 18239 (copy enclosed).  Also enclosed is the FPPC
Information Manual on Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the
Political Reform Act which explains the registration and reporting
requirements for lobbyists and lobbyist employers.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                   by:   Colleen McGee
                                        Political Reform Consultant


Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: What are the lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12 under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to issue formal advice because the requester is not authorized by the organization to seek advice, as the Commission cannot provide advice to third parties about the duties of another entity. General informational materials about lobbyist definitions and disclosure requirements are provided.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (informal advice request declined). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; content is embedded in letter body. Provides general informational guidance despite declining formal advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12 under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to issue formal advice because the requester is not authorized by the organization to seek advice, as the Commission cannot provide advice to third parties about the duties of another entity. General informational materials about lobbyist definitions and disclosure requirements are provided.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines John Kivi's request for informal advice regarding lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12, explaining that it cannot advise third parties about another entity's obligations, but provides general information about lobbyist definitions and regulatory requirements.
